description: Add a reflected lens-view op so the CLI can project any config-declared lens as a binned item-view — closes FGAP-073 at the general (all-lenses) altitude. A new op (proposed canonical name context-lens-view, sibling to context-edges-for-lens; confirm at plan time) keyed by --lensId reuses the shipped primitives loadLensView (packages/pi-context/src/lens-view.ts:43) + renderLensView (lens-view.ts:105) to return/render a lens's target items grouped into its declared bins. ADDITIVE per the FEAT-008 invariant: a NEW op reusing existing primitives; context-edges-for-lens + every other in-pi op byte-unchanged. The new op is slotted in the CLI grouped-help classifier (groupForOp in cli.ts — the exhaustive-disjoint guard forces this) and routed through the boundary read-cap (structureForRead) so a large view fail-closes. Implement via the canonical pipeline (explore -> plan -> agent -> verify, iterate to zero).
acceptance_criteria: CLI-PROVABLE (the originating friction, MUST work despite framework-gaps already exceeding 50KB): `pi-context context-lens-view --lensId gaps-by-status` returns the lens SUMMARY — every declared bin (identified / accepted / in-progress / closed / wontfix) with its item count (+ an uncategorized count) — ALWAYS under the 50KB cap regardless of substrate size. This is the scannable 'see open gaps' answer and must NOT fail-closed on the current >50KB framework-gaps.

CLI-PROVABLE (per-bin consumption, reusing the existing paging primitive — NOT a new mechanism): `pi-context context-lens-view --lensId gaps-by-status --bin identified [--offset N --limit M]` returns that bin's items (id/status/title), paged via the existing pageArray offset/limit/total/hasMore primitive (read-element.ts:188, the same primitive read-block-page uses) so any bin — including a large one — is fully consumable end-to-end. The originating 'see open gaps' works to completion despite framework-gaps >50KB.

CLI-PROVABLE: `--json` returns a single-parse structured object (summary: bins->counts; and the per-bin paged items when --bin is given), distinct from context-edges-for-lens's flat Edge[]; --json is the same payload --format json yields.

CLI-PROVABLE (lens-generic — the FGAP-073 general class): the summary + per-bin paths work for EACH of the four declared lenses (gaps-by-status, gaps-by-priority, tasks-by-status, features-by-status) with no lensId-specific code; an op-level test additionally exercises kind=composition + hand-curated dispatch via the shared loadLensView primitive.
CLI-PROVABLE (fails clean): an unknown --lensId, or an unknown --bin for a valid lens, emits a field-named error and a non-zero classifier exit code (not a crash, not exit 0).

CLI-PROVABLE (cap reuse, no new over-cap mechanism): the bin SUMMARY is always under cap; a single per-bin page that itself still exceeds 50KB fail-closes via the EXISTING uniform boundary (data:null / complete:false / truncated:true) carrying the standard overCapDirective to narrow --limit — i.e. the op reuses structureForRead + the boundary cap + pageArray, introducing no new pagination/continuation mechanism (per the explore: over-cap handling is already generalized + reusable).

CLI-PROVABLE (discoverability auto-covered): `pi-context context-lens-view --help` renders the best-of-breed per-op template (SYNOPSIS + typed flags incl. --lensId/--bin/--offset/--limit + EXAMPLES + RELATED + footer) and `--help --format json` emits the structured help model; the op carries authored examples[] (the gaps-by-status summary + a --bin invocation); the op appears in grouped `pi-context --help` under its derived group (slotted in groupForOp; the exhaustive-disjoint guard stays green).

Additive + gates: in-pi ops byte-unchanged except the new op (reuses loadLensView/renderLensView + pageArray; context-edges-for-lens + all others unchanged); `npx tsx scripts/parity-check.ts` exit 0 (the op<->CLI parity gate + the {json}-content-cap gate auto-cover the new op); `npm run build` + `npm run check` + `npm test` fail 0 with op-level tests (summary-under-cap on a >50KB lens, per-bin paging correctness via pageArray, per-lens binning across all four lenses + composition dispatch, unknown-lens + unknown-bin errors).

Closure: FGAP-073 closed by this task (verification_verifies_item + complete-task); canonical pipeline green — the runtime demo IS the CLI commands in the criteria above (esp. the summary + per-bin paths proving the >50KB gaps lens is fully consumable), plus a fresh adversarial probe re-deriving lens-generality, the summary-always-under-cap guarantee, per-bin paging reuse, and additivity.
notes: Addresses FGAP-073 (general altitude: all four target-lenses + composition/hand-curated; gaps-by-status the trigger). Reuses loadLensView (lens-view.ts:43) + renderLensView (lens-view.ts:105) + pageArray (read-element.ts:188) — exists-but-unexposed, additive reflection not new machinery. EXPLORE FINDING (over-cap output): the substrate's over-cap model is already generalized + reusable (single structureForRead/truncateHead choke point + boundedJsonOutput/renderOpResultText boundary backstops + the single pageArray paging primitive + per-op overCapDirective + the parity-check {json}-content-cap gate) — NO new reading-pagination mechanism is warranted. Because framework-gaps ALREADY exceeds 50KB, the lens-view op MUST consume by bin: a summary (counts, always under cap) + per-bin item paging via pageArray, NOT a whole-view render that would fail-closed on the originating gaps lens. The residual general edge (a single atomic unit >50KB with no sub-continuation) is a deliberate fail-closed design stance with no current concrete instance — surfaced per gap-explore-surfaces-class, not filed. Adjacent to FEAT-004 (config-driven state-derivation), distinct (lens projection). docs-surface-sync: the new op's description/promptSnippet + examples[] are user-facing; README/SKILL audit + CHANGELOG [Unreleased].
